title: Features, scope, and planning
intro: Quick check on the definitions above.
feedbackIncorrect: Review the definitions — try again on the next page if needed.

questions.prompt: In software product development, what best describes a feature?
choices: A distinct, user-facing capability or functionality that adds value by addressing needs or improving experience
Any internal refactor or dependency upgrade, regardless of user impact
A document that lists every bug ever reported in the product

questions.prompt: When you describe a feature for planning or communication, what should you emphasize?
choices: What it does and why it matters—not the details of how it will be implemented
The specific programming language and hosting provider you will use
Only the database indexes and API latency targets
Unplanned or additional tasks and requirements added after the initial scope is set
The approved list of goals and deliverables agreed at kickoff
The practice of removing features to ship faster

questions.prompt: How do product owners typically decide which feature to prioritize next?
choices: Solely by which one has the most lines of planned code
By analyzing user needs, business goals, impact, feedback, market trends, and technical feasibility—aligned with the product vision
By always choosing the feature that was requested most recently, regardless of strategy

questions.prompt: What does FERPA (Family Educational Rights and Privacy Act) primarily protect?
choices: The privacy of student education records, including rights to access and amend them, with limits on disclosing personally identifiable information without written permission except where allowed
Copyright and patent rights for software developed in university research labs
Minimum technical standards for learning management systems and gradebook APIs
